title: Unlived Garden Growth
description: A single procedural growth unit from the garden of unlived futures. It combines a dark branching stem, translucent ribbon leaves, a multi-petal probability bloom, suspended seed memories, and root filaments that never fully touch the ground. The asset feels botanical without copying any real-world species.
narrativeFunction: These growths make unrealized lives feel fertile rather than empty. Dense variation creates the garden canopy, while individual hero plants can carry a specific unchosen memory. Their slow reaching motion contrasts archive rigidity, bridge repetition, and the severity of probability monoliths.

orientation.originMeaning: The origin is the nominal root center projected onto the ground plane even though the visible roots hover above and below this point.

silhouette.primary: A tall curved stem forking into three upper branches supporting one dominant open bloom and two smaller incomplete buds.
silhouette.secondary: Four to seven ribbon leaves spiral around the stem at uneven intervals and create broad directional sweeps.
silhouette.tertiary: Seed memories, vein emission, floating root threads, translucent petal edges, dew-like possibility nodes, unopened buds, and mineral stem scars add close detail.
silhouette.negativeSpace: Large spaces between ribbon leaves and between bloom petals remain open. The root area appears airy and suspended rather than forming a dense bush.
silhouette.recognitionTest: At distance the asset reads as an elegant impossible flowering plant reaching toward one chosen direction, not as coral, a tree, seaweed, or a generic particle emitter.

materials.surfaceDescription: Dark blue-green fibrous material with subtle metallic mineral veins, brighter stress points at branch forks, and matte worn areas near the root crown.
Thin semi-translucent leaves with pale cyan edge emission, darker central veins, subtle layered fibers, and a soft transparency gradient toward their tips.

materials.role: Hero flower, buds, and seed memories
materials.surfaceDescription: Layered violet petals with luminous rims, milky internal depth, warmer seed cores, and slight color change according to view angle.

components.detail: The stem tapers upward, thickens at forks, contains a narrow luminous mineral vein, and leans toward the hero bloom without becoming a simple arc.

components.role: Three upper branches carrying the hero bloom and incomplete buds.
components.detail: Branches emerge at different heights and directions, preserve visible fork swelling, and terminate in one open bloom and two less-developed outcomes.

components.role: Broad twisting leaves establishing the plant's secondary silhouette.
components.detail: Each leaf twists along its length, narrows to a translucent point, carries one dark central vein, and avoids regular radial distribution.

components.role: Seven separated petals forming the dominant probability bloom.
components.detail: Petals cup around an open center, vary in length and curl, expose luminous edges, and preserve visible gaps even when the flower breathes.

components.role: Small suspended possibility seeds beneath blooms and leaf junctions.
components.detail: Seeds are teardrop-shaped, hang from nearly invisible threads, contain a warmer core, and cluster near the least-developed branch.

components.role: Fine root filaments hovering above and below the nominal ground plane.
components.detail: Roots fan outward, taper sharply, contain tiny drifting soil-light particles, and stop before touching any visible ground surface.

assemblyOrder: Construct hero bloom and smaller buds
Suspend seed memories and floating root filaments
deformationRules: The main stem leans eight to twenty degrees toward a designated light or destination vector.
Ribbon leaves twist forty to one hundred ten degrees along their length.
Petals cup outward while preserving at least three centimeters from neighboring petals.
variationRules: Bloom petal count ranges from five to nine while the default hero growth uses seven.
Leaf count ranges from four to eight and hue shifts within the cyan-violet and muted blue-green family.
Seed-memory count ranges from five to twelve and clusters around one unchosen branch.

topology.target: Game-ready curve and custom-profile organic topology with riggable stem branches, detachable ribbon leaves, separated bloom petals, instanced seeds, and reusable root filaments.
topology.bevel: Use smooth stem and root profiles, thin leaf edge bevels, and soft petal thickness sufficient to catch rim light without appearing plastic.
topology.subdivision: Use moderate subdivision on hero petals and leaves, lower curve resolution on background growths, and preserve slight faceting in mineral stems.
topology.normals: Use smooth normals on leaves and petals while preserving deliberate mineral facets and fork swelling on the stem.
topology.uv: Generate longitudinal stem UVs, unique leaf vein coordinates, radial petal UVs, dedicated edge-emission masks, and reusable seed material islands.

animation.motionCharacter: Growths move as though following distant possibility rather than wind. The stem reaches slowly, leaves answer with delayed twists, petals open by a few degrees, and seed cores brighten in sequence.
constraints: The hero bloom never closes completely.
Leaf and petal geometry never intersects the main stem during the loop.

collision.description: Use one narrow capsule around the lower main stem for hero growths. Background canopy instances use no collision.

sockets.purpose: Ground or floating garden placement point.
Camera, interaction, or memory-content focus at the dominant bloom.
Optional narrative object attached to the least-developed branch.

orthographicGuidance.front: Show the reaching main stem, three-branch crown, one dominant bloom, uneven ribbon leaves, and airy floating roots.
orthographicGuidance.side: Reveal leaf twist, branch depth separation, petal cup depth, and the fact that roots stop before ground contact.
orthographicGuidance.top: Make asymmetrical branch directions, open petal gaps, and nonuniform leaf spiral readable.
orthographicGuidance.threeQuarter: Use bloom-level light to reveal translucent edges, warm seed cores, and the dark mineral structure of the stem.
orthographicGuidance.scaleCue: Include a standing human beside the growth; the hero bloom sits slightly above head height.

validation: The default growth contains one dominant bloom, two incomplete buds, and three upper branches.
At least four large negative spaces remain between ribbon leaves from hero view.
Visible root filaments terminate above the ground plane.
Hero bloom petals stay separated and expose a readable open center.
Idle reaching motion preserves the root pivot and avoids self-intersection.
LOD2 retains the reaching stem, hero bloom, two leaf sweeps, and root direction.
Hero-focus and root-placement sockets align with intended vertical orientation.
